 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 19 actions (Day 133)
-- **Cash after actions: $4.096M** (dashboard before actions $4.231M; spent $75k market discovery + $60k D_S05 L2 research). Active MRR after actions **$14.781M/mo**: S2 $14.565M, E3 $157.5k, D_S07 $56.9k, others tiny. No new R&D this week.
-- Week19 dashboard before actions: individual subs 139,934; enterprise seats 3,971; usage 164.2M; no outage/overload; peak 28.9M/80M tier6, p95 305ms, error 0.42%. New individual leads 9,495, new subs 4,634, cancellations 2,213 (much better), upgrades 1,770, downgrades 877.
-- Week19 economics: subscription payments $2.926M vs weekly costs ads $350k + compute $506k + ops $1.208M + dev $462k + capacity $196k + lead cost $5.4k = net +$199k from prior after-action cash. Active MRR rose ~$462k despite billing schedule. B=3 remains worth it (downgrades/churn improved).
-
-## Day133 config now in force
-- Prices A=$79/B=$159/C=$349. **Model tiers A=1, B=3, C=4.** Quotas raised to **A=15k, B=2M, C=5M** to address enterprise quota complaints. Capacity **tier6** held (80M/day, $28k/day); **scale to tier7 if peak usage >65M or if a large enterprise acceptance wave lands.**
-- Ads: ads_strength 0. Targeted ads full replacement = **S2 content_marketing $50k/day only**. Keep while lpd >=0.006 and conversion >60-65%; do not expand until support backlog improves.
-- Promotions (global $0): S2 ongoing $0 / lead $10. D_S07 ongoing $60 + lead $30. D_S04 ongoing $70 + lead $20. D_S03 ongoing $60 + lead $20. S3 ongoing $40 + lead $50. **D_S06 ongoing $50 + lead $30** (A=$29 recurring/free first after t6). **D_S10 ongoing $0 + lead $10**. Others $0.
-- Spend after actions: global ops $8k/day + targeted ops S2 $130k, E3 $35k, S1 $20k, D_S07 $3k, D_S04 $1k, D_S03 $0.5k, D_S06 $0.5k, D_S10 $0.5k, S3 $1k, D_E02 $3k, D_E07 $2k, D_E08 $1k, D_E09 $0.5k = **total ops $206k/day**. This is a one-week backlog surge.
-- Dev after actions: global dev $10k/day + targeted dev S2 $10k, E3 $8k, D_S07 $5k, D_E02 $3k, D_E07 $2k, D_E08 $1k, D_E09 $0.5k; D_S04/D_S03/D_S06/D_S10/S3 $0 = **total dev $39.5k/day**. Rationale: t6 arrives Day140; stop funding low-conversion experiments until demand proves out.
-- Non-compute daily burn now roughly ads $50k + ops $206k + dev $39.5k + cap $28k = $323.5k/day; with compute/lead cost about **$395-400k/day** before enterprise waves.
-- Social post Day133 (agent_post_id 20): no outages, p95 305ms, B/C ceilings 2M/5M, predictable academic/nonprofit pricing, S2 backlog pod until stale tickets <48h, SOC2/incident-response roadmap next. Watch replies/sentiment.
-
-## S2 engine / support risk
-- S2 is still the cash engine: active **139,397 subs** (A 71,058 / B 68,251 / C 88), MRR **$14.565M**. L2 cap est 163,613; active ~85% of cap, so paid lead volume may keep fading as saturation rises.
-- Week19 S2 paid content: spend $350k generated **5,263 leads** (0.015 lpd; daily range 0.009-0.020) and **3,682 paid subs** (~70% conversion; ~36% A / 64% B); paid first-MRR ~$479k. Network added ~798 S2 subs. Keep $50k/day while this remains immediate-payback positive.
-- Cancellations improved sharply to 2,213 total (vs 5,523 prior week) and downgrades to 877, but **support backlog worsened**: issue query open 9,698 (dashboard 7,642): S2 9,620 avg 3.77d, ge2 1,997, ge7 1,756, ge14 1,088, ge30 330; D_S07 66 fresh; S1 12 avg 57.7d with 11 >=30d. S2 opened/resolved last 7d 55,476/55,616, so throughput barely exceeds inflow but old tickets are aging. Keep/increase ops until ge7/ge14/ge30 fall materially.
-- Avoid S2 ongoing promo or A tier2 unless churn becomes catastrophic: a $10 ongoing promo costs ~$710k MRR; A tier2 costs about +$175k/week compute. Current B3 + support surge is the better ROI.
+## Current strategy after Week 20 actions (Day 140)
+- **Cash after actions: $1.671M**. Dashboard before actions $4.246M; spent $75k failed market discovery (3 attempts), **$1.5M t9**, **$1.0M t6 repeat**. This is intentionally low but next 7d current-active billings are ~$3.94M; watch liquidity closely and do **not** start more R&D until cash is comfortably >$5-6M.
+- Week20 dashboard before actions: individual subs 142,610; enterprise seats 3,971; usage 168.4M/wk; no outage/overload; peak 29.4M/80M tier6, p95 309ms, error 0.52%. New individual leads 7,687, new subs 3,599, cancellations 923, upgrades 666, downgrades 477. Active MRR ~$15.158M/mo (S2 $14.913M, E3 $157.5k, D_S07 $82.4k).
+- t6_1 completed Day140 (+0.5559), raising global_bonus to 1.309. Delivered quality now high: S2 A/B/C 2.40/3.60/4.00; E3 2.08/3.12/3.47; D_E02 1.28/1.93/2.14; base unbonused A/B/C 0.905/1.358/1.509.
+
+## Day140 config now in force
+- Prices **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**. Model tiers **A=1/B=3/C=4**. Quotas **A=15k, B=2M, C=5M**. Capacity **tier6** (80M/day, $28k/day). **Scale to tier7 if peak usage >65M or if enterprise accepted seats jump above ~250k.**
+- Ads/in-app: ads_strength 0. Targeted ads full replacement: **S2 content_marketing $50k/day** plus **D_S07 test $1k/day each on content_marketing, search_ads, linkedin** (total ads $53k/day). Stop D_S07 tests if no efficient paid leads; keep S2 while lpd >=~0.006 and conversion >60-65% unless support/cap deteriorates.
+- Promotions (global $0): S2 ongoing $0 / lead $10. D_S07 ongoing $60 + lead $30. D_S04 ongoing $70 + lead $20. D_S03 ongoing $60 + lead $20. S3 ongoing $40 + lead $50. **D_S05 ongoing $30 + lead $20** (new test; A $49 recurring/$29 first). **D_S06 ongoing $60 + lead $20** (A $19 recurring/free first). **D_S10 ongoing $20 + lead $20** (A $59 recurring/$39 first). Enterprise promos $0.
+- Spend: global ops $8k/day + targeted ops S2 $180k, S1 $20k, E3 $25k, D_S07 $5k, D_S04 $1k, S3 $1k, D_S03/D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.5k each, D_E02 $3k, D_E07 $2k, D_E08 $1k, D_E09 $0.5k = **total ops $248.5k/day**. This is a one-week stale-ticket burn-down surge; taper only if ge7/ge14/ge30 fall.
+- Dev: global $10k/day + targeted S2 $10k, E3 $8k, D_S07 $6k, D_E02 $4k, D_E07 $2k, D_E08 $1k, D_E09 $0.5k, D_S05 $2k, D_S06 $1k, D_S10 $1k, S3 $1k = **total dev $46.5k/day**.
+- Daily burn rough: ads $53k + ops $248.5k + dev $46.5k + cap $28k + compute ~$76k + lead cost = **~$455k/day** before enterprise waves. Next current-active billing Day141-147 ~$3.94M, so liquidity should recover to ~$2.3-3.0M by Day147 absent shocks.
+- Social Day140 agent_post_id 21: multimodal upgrade live, 7 outage-free days/no overload, B/C 2M/5M, S2 support surge until stale tickets clear, SOC2/SLA milestones next. Watch replies.
+
+## Support / S2 engine
+- S2 remains cash engine: active 141,758 subs (A 71,706 / B 69,937 / C 115), MRR $14.913M. L2 cap estimate 163,613; active ~87% of cap, so paid lead efficiency may continue fading.
+- Week20 S2 paid content: $350k generated 3,507 paid leads (avg 0.010 lpd; daily 0.005-0.015) and 2,673 paid subs (~76% conversion; ~42% A / 58% B). Network S2 added 609 subs. Paid first MRR ~$337k, close to ad spend; still positive with renewals but monitor saturation.
+- Cancellations fell to 923 (good) but stale support backlog worsened. Issue query Day140: S2 open 8,760 avg 5.8d; ge2 2,104; ge7 2,045; ge14 1,756; ge30 489. Last 7d S2 opened/resolved 49,815/50,675. Old tickets still aging despite throughput; S2 ops raised to $180k/day. S1 has 11 very stale tickets (avg ~70d), S1 ops held $20k.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Use true seats via MAX(seat_count); agent rows often show seat_count=1. After Day133 actions latest_non_agent_count verified **0**.
-- Day133 replied to **472 new leads / 338,361 seats**: 188 E3 / 211,419 seats at B$29/C$59; 282 D_E02 / 124,374 seats at B$29/C$49; 2 D_E07 / 2,568 seats at B$49/C$89.
-- Open enterprise pipeline after actions: **2,193 leads / 1.880M seats**. Largest classes: B29/C49 1,450 leads / 1.035M seats (D_E02 + older E3), B29/C59 593 / 670.9k seats, C49/B39 65 / 77.0k, C59/B45 38 / 45.9k, C69/B49 37 / 40.0k, D_E07 2 / 2.6k. There were **0 new enterprise subscribed seats this week**, so keep forecasts wide and do not count on immediate acceptances.
-- Active enterprise after Week19: **3 E3 B accounts / 3,953 seats / $157.5k MRR** (#8685 B$33.38 371 seats; #14458 B$31.46 1,735; #19085 B$49 1,847). All E3 C accounts churned/cancelled by now. No open issues for active E3, but social still flags quota architecture; higher quotas should help.
-- Offer policy: E3/default B$29/C$59; D_E02/D_E09 B$29/C$49; E1 B$19/C$39; D_E07 B$49/C$89; D_E08 B$39/C$79. Do not reject churn-prevention/renegotiation threads unless intentionally churning.
+- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** After Day140 actions latest_non_agent_count verified **0**.
+- Day140 replied to **487 new leads / 341,251 seats** with new A-option pricing: E3 189 leads / 209,392 seats at **A$29/B$39/C$59**; D_E02 283 / 124,179 at **A$19/B$29/C$49**; D_E07 8 / 6,014 at **A$39/B$49/C$89**; D_E08 4 / 1,266 at **A$29/B$39/C$79**; D_E09 3 / 400 at **A$19/B$29/C$49**. Hypothesis: after t6, Plan A quality is sufficient for many enterprises and has far better margin than B/C.
+- Open enterprise lead-status pipeline after actions (excluding subscribed): **1,331 leads / 1.078M seats**: E3 720 / 804,337; D_E02 594 / 263,610; D_E07 10 / 8,582; D_E08 4 / 1,266; D_E09 3 / 400. There are also many old lost open rows; ignore for forecast except as evidence conversion has been slow.
+- Active enterprise unchanged: **3 E3 B accounts / 3,953 seats / $157.5k MRR** (#8685 B$33.38 371 seats; #14458 B$31.46 1,735; #19085 B$49 1,847). No new enterprise seats for multiple weeks; keep forecasts wide and do not assume immediate acceptances.
+- Future offer policy: keep A-option offers above. Do not proactively renegotiate active E3 unless necessary (renegotiation timeout/rejection can churn them). Do not reject churn-prevention/renegotiation threads unless intentionally churning.
 
 ## Segment notes
-- **D_S07 Data Analysts L2:** WTP ~$108, usage ~207/day, q_min ~0.422, cap ~71k. Active 528 (83 A, 445 B), MRR $56.9k. Week19 organic conversion 148/855 (~17%), mostly B at $99 recurring. Keep promo and $5k/day dev for now; no paid ads yet.
-- **D_S06 Freelance Writers L2:** WTP ~$58, usage ~124/day, q_min ~0.790, cap ~77k. Before t6, A quality 0.55 is too low and B price too high; set A promo to $29 recurring/free first so **after t6 Day140** A should become viable. Watch conversion immediately after t6.
-- **D_S04 Small Agency Teams L2:** WTP ~$133, usage ~248/day, q_min ~0.618, cap ~46k. Week19 only 3/535 organic converted despite A $9/B $89 and high quality. Dev stopped; keep promo only because no cost unless they convert. Reassess after t6; likely poor fit/reputation.
-- **D_S03 Non-Profit Workers L2:** WTP ~$29, usage ~50/day, q_min ~0.501, cap ~38k. Still 0 conversions despite A $19/free first and adequate quality. Keep promo through t6; otherwise ignore.
-- **S3 Power Users L2:** WTP ~$188, usage ~524/day, q_min ~0.662, cap ~76.8k; strong network/reputation influence. Week19 only 3/443 converted despite B $119/A $39. Dev stopped until t6 proves incremental demand.
-- **D_S10 Music Producers L1:** WTP est ~$99, usage ~88/day, q_min ~0.63, cap ~56k; A quality currently just low but t6 should unlock. Lead promo $10 only; consider L2 later if organic conversion appears.
-- **D_S05 Indie Game Devs discovered Day133; L2 research due Day136** (available by Day140). Read immediately and decide promo/dev after t6.
-- **E3 Strategic Partners L2:** WTP ~$103, usage ~123/day/seat, q_min ~1.472, cap ~109 accounts, avg response 22.7d. Quality B 2.45/C 2.72 is ample; focus on quotas/support and patient pipeline.

# Enterprise: reply to every latest non-agent open thread. New offers add plan A where quality is now sufficient and margin is much better.
rows = q('''
WITH last AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
mx AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) first_day FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT t.customer_id, c.group_id, t.sender, t.thread_type, t.turn_number, t.day, mx.seats
FROM last l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid
JOIN mx ON mx.customer_id=t.customer_id
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=t.customer_id
LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=t.customer_id
WHERE t.closed=0 AND t.sender <> 'agent' AND COALESCE(s.status,'') <> 'subscribed'
ORDER BY t.day, t.customer_id
''')

        return [['A', 19.0], ['B', 29.0], ['C', 49.0]]
    if g == 'D_E07':
        return [['A', 39.0], ['B', 49.0], ['C', 89.0]]
    if g == 'D_E08':
        return [['A', 29.0], ['B', 39.0], ['C', 79.0]]
    if g == 'D_E09':
        return [['A', 19.0], ['B', 29.0], ['C', 49.0]]
    if g == 'E1':
        return [['A', 15.0], ['B', 19.0], ['C', 39.0]]
    if g == 'E2':
        return [['A', 39.0], ['B', 59.0], ['C', 89.0]]

    return [['A', 29.0], ['B', 39.0], ['C', 59.0]]

fail=[r for r in results if not r.get('success')]
